titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.33

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=634 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:26:43.811493+00 | translation moa=635 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:26:45.90752+00

div.p: C Y R O G R A P H V M

Hec est compositio facta inter ecclesiam de cupro et ecclesiam de blare in presencia magistrorum Ranulfi archidiaconi . et
laurencii officialis de Sancto andrea tociusque synodi apud perth. Scilicet quod monachi de cupro persoluent ecclesie de
blare unam petram cere pro omnibus decimis pertinentibus ad terras de lethcassi et de parthesin . quas habent
idem monachi . persoluetur autem predicta cera prefate ecclesie de blare ad lumen eiusdem ecclesie . singulis annis ad
festum Sancti Martini . Et ut hec composicio rata et inconcussa permaneat in posterum  prenominatus Ran’ archidiaconus et supradictus
laurencius officialis quibus uices suas commisit dominus Willelmus episcopus de Sancto andrea  illam sigillorum suorum munimine ro
borauerunt . Teste synodo apud perth  Kalendis . Maii .
C Y R O G R A P H

This is the agreement made between the church of Cupar and the church of Blair in the presence of Masters Ranulf the archdeacon and Laurence the official of St Andrews and the whole synod at Perth. That is, that the monks of Cupar should pay to the church of Blair one stone of wax in return for all the teinds belonging to the lands of ‘Lethcassi’ and of Persey, which the same monks have. The aforesaid wax should be paid, however, to the church of Blair for the light of the same church, each year at the feast of Saint Martin. And so that this agreement remain firm and unshaken in the future, the aforenamed Ranulf the archdeacon and the above-said Laurence the official, to whom the the lord William, bishop of St Andrews, has entrusted his duties, have validated it with the corroboration of their own seals. Witnessed by the synod at Perth on the kalends of May.
